Handles revisions within the document.

Exposes section-level properties.

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Word.WordDocument.AddChart(System.String,System.Boolean,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Adds the chart to the document. The type of chart is determined by the type of data passed in. You can use multiple: .AddBar() to add a bar chart .AddLine() to add a line chart .AddPie() to add a pie chart .AddArea() to add an area chart .AddScatter() to add a scatter chart .AddRadar() to add a radar chart .AddBar3D() to add a 3-D bar chart. .AddPie3D() to add a 3-D pie chart. .AddLine3D() to add a 3-D line chart. You can't mix and match the types of charts, except bar and line which can coexist in a combo chart.

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Word.WordDocument.AddComboChart(System.String,System.Boolean,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Creates a chart ready for combining bar and line series. Use to supply category labels and then call or to add data. The call to AddChartAxisX must be performed before adding any series so both chart types share the same axes.

member.name: P:OfficeIMO.Word.WordChart.PrivateTitle
member.summary:
Holds the title of the chart until we can add it to the chart
Note: the title can't really be added to a chart before we know what type of chart it is Since we don't know what type of chart it is until we add the first Pie, Bar, Line or Area we need to wait until then to add the title This is why we have a separate property for the title, but the method to add the title is in the WordChart class

member.summary: Adds a 3D line chart series to the chart.
member.param: The data values for the series
member.remarks: KNOWN ISSUE: Line3DChart currently fails OpenXML schema validation with the error: "The element has unexpected child element 'ser'". This appears to be a discrepancy between Microsoft's documentation (which shows Line3DChart supports LineChartSeries) and the actual OpenXML schema validator implementation. This issue persists regardless of element ordering and may indicate that Line3DChart does not actually support data series in the current OpenXML specification.

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Word.WordTable.NormalizeForOnline
member.summary: Normalizes this table for online viewers by updating tblGrid only. Does not change authoring semantics (tblW, width type, positioning). Skips pure-Auto tables (keeps Word's native behavior): - no table preferred width (or Auto), - no column width type set, - and all cells carry only the library's default tcW (DXA 2400).

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Word.WordTable.EnsureFirstRowTopBorder
member.summary: Ensures the parent cell of a nested table has at least the provided top/bottom padding (cell margins) so the inner table does not touch the borders vertically. No effect for non-nested tables. Applied conservatively (only when current value is null or 0).

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Word.WordTable.ConvertHorizontalMergesToGridSpan
member.summary: Converts w:hMerge (restart/continue) patterns to w:gridSpan and removes the continued cells. Many online viewers ignore hMerge but support gridSpan.

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Word.WordTable.AutoFitToWindow
member.summary: Sets the table to AutoFit to Window (100% width)

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Word.WordTable.SetFixedWidth(System.Int32)
member.summary: Sets the table to Fixed Width with the specified percentage

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Word.WordTable.SetColumnWidthsPercentage(System.Int32[])
member.summary: Sets column widths using percentages. Values are scaled proportionally when they don't sum to 100.
member.param: Column percentages in any positive ratio.

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Word.WordTable.GetCurrentLayoutType
member.summary: Gets the current table layout mode based on its properties

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Word.WordTable.SetWidthPercentage(System.Int32)
member.summary: Sets the table width to a percentage of the window width

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Word.WordTable.Clone
member.summary: Creates a deep copy of the current table and inserts it after the table.

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Word.WordTable.CloneAfterSelf
member.summary: Clones the table and inserts the clone after the current table.

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Word.WordTable.CloneBeforeSelf
member.summary: Clones the table and inserts the clone before the current table.

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Word.WordTable.Clone(OfficeIMO.Word.WordParagraph,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Clones the table and inserts it relative to the specified paragraph.
member.param: If true inserts after the paragraph, otherwise before.

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Word.WordTable.Clone(OfficeIMO.Word.WordTable,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Clones the table and inserts it relative to another table.
member.param: If true inserts after the table, otherwise before.

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Word.WordTable.RefreshTblGridFromColumnWidths
member.summary: Rebuilds the table grid (w:tblGrid) using DXA widths derived from current column width values/types and the table preferred width. Many consumers (Word Online, Google Docs) rely primarily on tblGrid when laying out columns and ignore cell tcW percentages. Keeping tblGrid in sync avoids the observed 50/50 column issue.

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Word.WordTable.GetBestAvailableColumnWidths(System.Nullable{DocumentFormat.OpenXml.Wordprocessing.TableWidthUnitValues}@,System.Int32)
member.summary: Attempts to derive a complete set of column widths and their unit by scanning rows. Prefer any row that has widths for all columns; fall back to the first row with any widths.

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Word.WordTable.RefreshGrid
member.summary: Exposed for internal callers in this assembly that change table structure (e.g., InsertColumn) and need to update the tblGrid.

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Word.WordTable.EstimateTableWidthInDxa
member.summary: Estimates the effective table width in DXA (twips) based on Table.Width/WidthType and the section page width/margins.

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Word.WordTable.EstimateContentAreaWidthInDxa
member.summary: Returns the estimated text area width (page width minus left/right margins) in DXA. Uses the first section when the owning section can't be easily resolved.

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Word.WordTable.EstimateContainingCellContentWidthInDxa
member.summary: Estimates the available content width of the containing table cell (for nested tables). Falls back to page content width when structure cannot be determined.

member.name: P:OfficeIMO.Word.WordFieldParser.Instructions
member.summary: Gets the positional instructions extracted from the field code.

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Word.WordFieldParser.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary:
Fieldcodes consist of several components - similar to URLs - but OpenXML only handles the whole string. This class provides an type-based interpretation of those strings. The general syntax is: FIELDCODE [Instructions] [switches] [format_switches]
The fieldcode is a string without whitespace
The instructions are optionally, though some field codes (like ask) require them. Instructions are positional statements.
Switches can be found alone (like \h) or with a parameter (like \h 1223 or like \t "what so ever")
Format switches starts with \* or \#. The latter is not implemented yet. It appears only the last format switch will be interpreted by Word - apart from MERGEFORMAT. However, in the effort to keep all information, all switches will be exported.

member.name: T:OfficeIMO.Word.WordParagraph
member.summary: Represents a paragraph within a Word document.
Implements equality comparison for paragraphs.
Provides accessors for paragraph formatting.
Contains private helper methods for WordParagraph.
Contains public methods for editing paragraphs.
Adds revision-related utilities.
Exposes run-level helpers for WordParagraph.
Provides run enumeration helpers for .
Manages run property settings.
Offers methods to modify run properties.

member.name: P:OfficeIMO.Word.WordParagraph.Text
member.summary:
Gets or sets the text for this run. Text-wrapping breaks (null or BreakValues.TextWrapping) are surfaced as "
" in the returned string so callers receive the same representation on every platform. The setter accepts any mix of "
", "", or "
" and normalizes them to "
" before updating the OpenXML elements. Non text-wrapping breaks—such as page or column breaks—are represented using the Unicode line separator character (' ') so that text operations (for example find/replace) can preserve their positions. When the text is modified those breaks are re-inserted at their original locations.

member.summary: Gets the identifier of the paragraph style, if any.

member.name: P:OfficeIMO.Word.WordParagraph.ParagraphAlignment
member.summary: Alignment aka Paragraph Alignment. This element specifies the paragraph alignment which shall be applied to text in this paragraph. If this element is omitted on a given paragraph, its value is determined by the setting previously set at any level of the style hierarchy (i.e.that previous setting remains unchanged). If this setting is never specified in the style hierarchy, then no alignment is applied to the paragraph.

member.name: P:OfficeIMO.Word.WordParagraph.VerticalCharacterAlignmentOnLine
member.summary: Text Alignment aka Vertical Character Alignment on Line. This element specifies the vertical alignment of all text on each line displayed within a paragraph. If the line height (before any added spacing) is larger than one or more characters on the line, all characters are aligned to each other as specified by this element. If this element is omitted on a given paragraph, its value is determined by the setting previously set at any level of the style hierarchy (i.e.that previous setting remains unchanged). If this setting is never specified in the style hierarchy, then the vertical alignment of all characters on the line shall be automatically determined by the consumer.

member.summary: Adds the chart to the document. The type of chart is determined by the type of data passed in. You can use multiple: .AddBar() to add a bar chart .AddLine() to add a line chart .AddPie() to add a pie chart .AddArea() to add an area chart .AddScatter() to add a scatter chart .AddRadar() to add a radar chart .AddBar3D() to add a 3-D bar chart. .AddPie3D() to add a 3-D pie chart. .AddLine3D() to add a 3-D line chart. You can't mix and match the types of charts, except bar and line which can coexist in a combo chart.

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Word.WordParagraph.AddComboChart(System.String,System.Boolean,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Creates a chart ready for combining bar and line series. Use to supply category labels and then call or to add data. AddChartAxisX must be called before adding any series so that both chart types share the same axes.

To make sure that FontFamily works correctly on special characters we change the RunFonts.Ascii and RunFonts.HighAnsi, EastAsia and ComplexScript properties If you want to set different FontFamily for HighAnsi, EastAsia and ComplexScript please use FontFamilyHighAnsi, FontFamilyEastAsia or FontFamilyComplexScript in proper order (to overwrite given FontFamily)

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Word.WordSection.CopySectionProperties(DocumentFormat.OpenXml.Wordprocessing.SectionProperties,DocumentFormat.OpenXml.Wordprocessing.SectionProperties)
member.summary: This method moves headers and footers and title page to section before it. It also copies all other parts of sections (PageSize,PageMargin and others) to section before it. This is because headers/footers when applied to section apply to the rest of the document unless there are headers/footers on next section. On the other hand page size doesn't apply to other sections and word uses default values.

member.name: T:OfficeIMO.Word.WordSmartArt
member.summary: Represents a SmartArt diagram in a .

member.name: P:OfficeIMO.Word.WordSmartArt.NodeCount
member.summary: Gets the number of editable node paragraphs in the SmartArt. Nodes are detected as points that have a text body and no explicit type (not doc/parTrans/sibTrans/pres).

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Word.WordSmartArt.GetNodeText(System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns the text of the node at the specified 0-based index.
member.param: The 0-based position of the SmartArt node whose text should be retrieved.
member.returns: The concatenated text content of the targeted node, or an empty string when the node has no text.

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Word.WordSmartArt.SetNodeText(System.Int32,System.String)
member.summary: Sets the text of the node at the specified 0-based index while preserving the paragraph end run properties.
member.param: The 0-based position of the SmartArt node whose content should be replaced.
The replacement text to apply to the node.

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Word.WordSmartArt.ReplaceTexts(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.String})
member.summary: Replaces node texts sequentially starting at index 0. If more texts are provided than nodes, extras are ignored. If fewer texts are provided, remaining nodes are left unchanged.
member.param: The ordered replacement strings to apply to each node in turn.

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Word.WordSmartArt.ReplaceTexts(System.String[])
member.summary: Convenience overload for replacing node texts sequentially using a parameter array.
member.param: The ordered replacement strings to apply to each node in turn.

member.name: M:OfficeIMO.Word.WordSmartArt.ReplaceTexts(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.String},System.Boolean,System.Boolean,System.Boolean,System.String,System.Nullable{System.Double})
member.summary: Replaces node texts sequentially while applying the same formatting to every replacement value. If more texts are provided than nodes, extras are ignored. If fewer are provided, remaining nodes are unchanged.
member.param: The ordered replacement strings to apply to each node in turn.
True to render each replacement in bold; otherwise false.
True to render each replacement in italics; otherwise false.
True to underline each replacement; otherwise false.
An optional RGB color value (for example, "FF0000") applied to each replacement, or null to keep the existing color.
An optional font size in points applied to each replacement, or null to keep the existing size.
